sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Mallorca are struggling, with no wins in their last 4 matches and a pattern of conceding in every game over their last 5. Athletic Club's form is also inconsistent, with only 1 win in their last 5 matches and no clean sheets in 4 games. Both teams have shown vulnerability defensively, setting the stage for a potentially open game.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Athletic Club have the upper hand, securing a 2-1 victory in their most recent match and a 1-1 draw prior. Historically, matches between these sides have been low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 8 meetings. Athletic remain unbeaten in 8 consecutive games against Mallorca.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Mallorca's Vedat Muriqi is a standout with 11 goals this season, ranking 2nd in La Liga, and could exploit Athletic's leaky defense. Athletic's Nico Williams, with his dribbling prowess (2.6 successful dribbles per game), poses a threat on the counter. Both teams struggle with possession (Mallorca 44.2%, Athletic similar), suggesting a battle in the midfield.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Mallorca's strength in aerial duels (52.4% win rate) and late scoring (8 goals in the 76-90 minute range) could be key at home. Athletic Club's tendency to score in the latter stages (24% of goals after the 76th minute) indicates they may push for a late winner. Expect a physical game with high foul counts, as both sides rank high in fouls committed.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Mallorca's home advantage and Sofascore's 43% probability of a home win, they are slight favorites, but Athletic's historical dominance in this fixture cannot be ignored. Mallorca's defensive frailties and Athletic's attacking inconsistencies suggest a draw is plausible. A tight, low-scoring match is likely, aligning with head-to-head trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Mallorca and Athletic Club ended in an exciting 2-2 draw at Estadi de Son Moix. Both teams showed significant attacking intent, with each side netting twice. Mallorca struck first through Vedat Muriqi early in the game, only for Athletic Club to respond swiftly with a goal from Unai Gómez. Muriqi added a second for the home side before Nico Williams equalized for Athletic Club right at the end of the half. The scoreline reflects a balanced encounter with both teams creating ample scoring opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Mallorca displayed a strong presence in the final third, managing several entries and creating big chances, though they missed a notable opportunity. Their aerial dominance and overall duel-winning ability gave them an edge in physical battles. Athletic Club, on the other hand, showed more intent in terms of shots on target and penalty area entries, but struggled with finishing as they missed a couple of big chances. Their ball possession was almost on par with Mallorca, indicating a closely contested midfield battle. However, Athletic Club's disciplinary record in the first half, with multiple yellow cards, could impact their approach in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: Vedat Muriqi has been the standout performer for Mallorca, scoring twice and posing a constant threat upfront. His ability to convert chances makes him a focal point for the home side’s attack. Sergi Darder also contributed significantly with an assist and key passes. For Athletic Club, Unai Gómez made a strong impact with a goal and an assist, while Nico Williams’ late equalizer showcased his knack for decisive moments. Gorka Guruzeta’s involvement in creating big chances suggests he could be pivotal if given more opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw frequent shifts in attacking momentum between the two sides. Athletic Club started with early pressure, pushing forward aggressively in the opening minutes. Mallorca responded with a strong spell of dominance shortly after, capitalizing on their attacking surge to take the lead. The momentum swung back to Athletic Club with a sustained period of control in the middle of the half, allowing them to level the score. Towards the latter stages, Mallorca regained the upper hand with a significant attacking push, only for Athletic Club to claw back with a late burst before the whistle.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to remain as open and competitive as the first, given both teams’ attacking output so far. Mallorca will aim to leverage their aerial strength and home advantage to dictate play, with Muriqi expected to be central to their efforts. Athletic Club, despite their disciplinary concerns, have shown they can penetrate the final third effectively and will look to capitalize on any defensive lapses. The game could hinge on which side tightens up defensively while maintaining their attacking threat.
